subject: Mathematics
title: The Stadium Paradox
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Stadium Paradox usable.

hook.text: Rows of soldiers march past each other in a stadium. Their relative speed creates a contradiction.

buildup.text: Zeno imagined rows moving in opposite directions at equal speed past a stationary row.

discovery.text: A moving soldier passes two stationary soldiers in the same time it passes four moving ones.

twist.text: Zeno concluded that half a time equals a whole time—an obvious absurdity meant to challenge assumptions.

climax.text: The paradox actually challenged the idea that time and space come in indivisible atoms.

punchline.text: Relative motion confused the ancients. It still bends minds today.

quiz.question: What did Zeno's stadium paradox challenge?
options: The idea that time and space are made of indivisible units
